title: The receiving stance
subtitle: A brief ritual for using the body's posture to signal readiness to receive money.
excerpt: A ritual practice aligned with Day 27 of the MONEY program. Use to shift from a body posture of bracing and protecting into one of open, grounded receiving.
sourceNotes: Original to homemade.education, drawing on the somatic tradition of using posture and breath as signals to the nervous system. Aligned with Day 27 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025).

practice.whenToUse: Use when you want to prepare the body to receive — before a big payment lands, before a negotiation, or any time the bracing response needs to be interrupted.

content.text: A brief ritual aligned with Day 27 of the MONEY program. The premise is somatic: the body has a default receiving posture (shoulders back, chest open, palms up) and a default bracing posture. This ritual uses posture and breath to move from one to the other. The transition is physical before it is emotional.
Stand or sit with your spine upright. Let your hands rest in your lap, palms down. Notice the posture your body is already in.
Turn your palms to face upward, resting on your thighs or with arms slightly open at your sides. This is the receiving position.
Drop your shoulders. Unclench your jaw. Let your chest soften open.
Take a slow breath in through the nose. Hold for two counts. Release fully through the mouth.
Say once, quietly: "I am open to receive."
Repeat the breath and the statement two more times.
Stay in the posture for one full minute after the final statement. Then return to your day.

Original to homemade.education, drawing on the somatic tradition of using posture and breath as signals to the nervous system. Aligned with Day 27 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
